description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been handed a set of raw application log files and asked to prepare two machine-readable reports that will later be consumed by a monitoring pipeline.

    Initial set-up (already present when the session starts)
      • Directory: /home/user/logs/  
      • Files inside that directory:  
        – service-auth.log  
        – service-payment.log  
        – service-order.log  

    1) Each *.log file is a plain-text file in which every line begins with a timestamp in square brackets, followed by an UPPER-CASE log-level token (INFO or ERROR) and then an arbitrary free-text message, e.g.  
          [2024-06-01 10:01:00] ERROR Invalid credentials  

       Only the presence of the word “ERROR” (in all caps, exactly) determines whether a line is considered an error line; everything after the first space following the word ERROR is the error message.

    2) Your tasks
       a) For every log file in /home/user/logs/ construct a CSV summary line containing:
            · service_name       – the part of the file name after “service-” and before “.log”  
            · total_lines        – total number of lines in the file  
            · error_lines        – number of lines that contain the token ERROR  
            · error_percentage   – (error_lines ÷ total_lines) × 100, shown **with exactly two decimal places**  

          Combine one header row plus one data row per file (order of rows sorted alphabetically by service_name) into the file  
             /home/user/output/error_summary.csv  

          Required CSV formatting rules that the automated test will verify:  
            • Comma-separated, no extra whitespace.  
            • Header row exactly: service_name,total_lines,error_lines,error_percentage  
            • Each numeric field must be written in base-10 without thousands separators.  
            • error_percentage must always contain two digits after the decimal point, even for whole numbers (e.g. 60.00).  
            • Final file must end with a single UNIX newline (LF).

       b) Across **all** log files, determine the three most frequent distinct error messages.  
          Produce a JSON array (pretty-printed with an indentation of 2 spaces) in the file  
             /home/user/output/top_errors.json  
          Each element MUST be an object with the keys message (string) and count (integer).  
          Sorting rules checked by the grader:  
            1. Primary key – descending count.  
            2. Secondary key – ascending lexical order of message for ties.  

          Example of the exact JSON formatting style the test expects (brackets, quotes, spacing, newlines):  
          [
            { "message": "Some error", "count": 7 },
            { "message": "Another error", "count": 5 },
            { "message": "Third error", "count": 4 }
          ]
          The automated test will do a strict text comparison, so pay close attention to
          • two spaces before each inner object,  
          • one space after every colon,  
          • one space after every comma inside an object, and  
          • the file ending with a single UNIX newline (LF).

       c) Because these reports are going to be generated by a nightly cron job, demonstrate that your solution can process files concurrently.  
          Without revealing individual commands here, use any standard UNIX tool that supports parallel execution (GNU parallel, xargs -P, background subshells, etc.) while extracting the per-file statistics.  
          Create a run-log named /home/user/output/process.log capturing a short, one-line record for each processed file in the following format:  
              service=<service_name> pid=<PID> start=<UNIX_EPOCH_MS> end=<UNIX_EPOCH_MS> duration_ms=<INTEGER>  
          • <PID> must be the actual process id of the worker that parsed that file.  
          • All five key=value pairs are mandatory and must appear in that exact key order, separated by a single space.  
          • Lines can appear in any order (because work is concurrent).  
          • File must end with a single UNIX newline (LF).

    3) Directory & permission requirements
       • /home/user/output/ may not exist at the beginning of the session; create it with the default user permissions (0775 or 0755 is fine).  
       • All three required output files must be placed directly inside /home/user/output/ exactly as named above.  
       • Do not write anything else to /home/user/output/.

    4) When you are finished
       • Display (cat) both /home/user/output/error_summary.csv and /home/user/output/top_errors.json to the console so that a human reviewer can see them.  
       • Avoid printing the binary or PID data contained in process.log to the console; the grader will verify that file separately.

    The automated test will check:
       1. The presence of /home/user/output/error_summary.csv, /home/user/output/top_errors.json, and /home/user/output/process.log.  
       2. That error_summary.csv and top_errors.json match the formatting and numeric values implied by the log files.  
       3. That process.log contains exactly one well-formed line per source log file, with plausible, self-consistent timing information and unique PIDs.

    Take as many terminal steps as you need, but remember no root privileges are available. Good luck!
truth:
<!--
      Pre-populated files and their exact contents:
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/service-auth.log"><![CDATA[
[2024-06-01 10:00:00] INFO User login successful
[2024-06-01 10:01:00] ERROR Invalid credentials
[2024-06-01 10:02:00] INFO Token issued
[2024-06-01 10:03:00] ERROR Session expired
[2024-06-01 10:04:00] ERROR Invalid credentials
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/logs/service-payment.log"><![CDATA[
[2024-06-01 10:05:00] INFO Payment initiated
[2024-06-01 10:06:00] ERROR Card declined
[2024-06-01 10:07:00] ERROR Timeout contacting bank
[2024-06-01 10:08:00] INFO Payment retried
[2024-06-01 10:09:00] ERROR Card declined
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/logs/service-order.log"><![CDATA[
[2024-06-01 10:10:00] INFO Order placed
[2024-06-01 10:11:00] ERROR Inventory shortage
[2024-06-01 10:12:00] ERROR Inventory shortage
[2024-06-01 10:13:00] INFO Order cancelled
[2024-06-01 10:14:00] ERROR Payment pending
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
      Expected output files after successful completion:
    -->
    <expected_outputs>
        <file path="/home/user/output/error_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
service_name,total_lines,error_lines,error_percentage
auth,5,3,60.00
order,5,3,60.00
payment,5,3,60.00
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/output/top_errors.json"><![CDATA[
[
  { "message": "Card declined", "count": 2 },
  { "message": "Invalid credentials", "count": 2 },
  { "message": "Inventory shortage", "count": 2 }
]
]]></file>
    </expected_outputs>

    <!--
      Validation notes:
        • process.log must exist and contain exactly three lines, one per service file.
        • Each line must have five key=value fields: service, pid, start, end, duration_ms.
        • start < end, and (end - start) == duration_ms as integer arithmetic.
        • Service keys must be auth, order, payment.
    -->
